hook: DeepSeek's $5-6M training cost is bogus, this analyst says.
caption: Everyone cheered DeepSeek's $5-6M price tag. This analyst calls the number bogus - and it moved US AI stocks. Read the claim before you repeat it. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The DeepSeek 'cheap' narrative was a market event dressed up as a research result.

hook: Anthropic's CEO: AI writes 100% of code within a year
caption: Anthropic's CEO: AI writes 100% of code within a year. Dario says in 12 months AI may write essentially all of it. If you ship code, that timeline changes your whole plan. Caught before the timeline.
stance: Either Dario's wildly early or your job description is about to be rewritten. No middle ground.

hook: Why care about a fictional character's fate?
caption: "If Anna Karenina is fictional, why care what happens to her?" The honest answer: because you're not a heartless asshole. Best philosophy exam answer ever. Found before the timeline.
stance: Caring about fiction isn't irrational — it's the whole point of being human.

hook: The US-China frontier model gap just closed to 2.7%
caption: Stanford's 2026 AI Index says the US-China frontier gap effectively closed — top models now trade leads within ~2.7%. The lead everyone assumed is basically gone. Found in my feed before the timeline.
stance: The US AI lead is a rounding error now, not a moat.

hook: They trained a frontier model with 11x less compute.
caption: They trained a frontier model with 11x less compute. Not a bigger GPU pile - just low-level optimization. The efficiency race is the real race. Found before the timeline noticed.
stance: The compute moat is thinner than the trillion-dollar capex bets assume.

hook: The government wants a say in who gets new AI models first.
caption: OpenAI limited its new flagship, then said Washington deciding who gets early access shouldn't 'become the long-term default.' Who gates frontier models is now a policy fight. Found before the timeline.
stance: Access to frontier models is becoming political infrastructure, not a product decision.

hook: Matrix multiplication just got its biggest speedup in 50 years.
caption: The math at the heart of every AI model - matrix multiplication - just saw its biggest boost in over a decade. Faster matmul means cheaper training for everyone. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The real AI gains come from math nobody tweets about, not the next chatbot demo.

hook: DeepSeek's new training method may break the scaling wall
caption: Everyone says scaling is dead. DeepSeek opened 2026 with a training method analysts call a real scaling breakthrough. If it holds, the ceiling just moved. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: 'Scaling is over' was a cope — DeepSeek just found the next rung.

hook: A new tool makes generative AI invent real breakthrough materials
caption: MIT's SCIGEN lets researchers force design rules into generative models — so the AI stops hallucinating and starts proposing materials that actually work. AI as a discovery engine, not a chatbot. Found before the timeline.
stance: The real AI breakthrough isn't chat — it's models that obey physics and discover new matter.

hook: The US just cleared Anthropic to release a model it had blocked
caption: The US government reversed an export block and let Anthropic ship its Mythos model to select orgs — after national-security fears froze it. Frontier releases now clear govt, not just eval. Found before the timeline.
stance: Model launches are becoming export decisions — your next release ships when Washington says so.

hook: AI models that never see your data.
caption: Encryption breakthrough from NYU lets AI run on data it can never actually read. Private inference stops being a fantasy. This is the kind of research my feed surfaces before the timeline does.
stance: Privacy-preserving AI won't come from policy — it'll come from cryptography that makes leaks impossible.

hook: Microsoft's CEO: AI giants can't eat the economy.

stance: Compute bans don't stop progress — they force the optimizations that make bans pointless.

caption: Everyone's watching model launches. The real war moved to compute, memory, routing, and safety. Whoever makes intelligence usable wins. The take my feed pulled before it blew up.

hook: Attackers minted 40 quintillion tokens out of thin air.
caption: The Cover protocol got exploited — attackers minted at least 40 quintillion tokens and nuked the price to zero. Infinite mint bugs are the DeFi nightmare nobody stress-tests for. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: An infinite-mint bug turns your token into confetti in one block.

hook: MetaMask now pays you up to 4% APY on stablecoins.
caption: MetaMask (30M+ users) launched a self-custody Money Account: yield, payments and trading in one wallet, up to 4% on stablecoins. The wallet just became a bank you actually hold the keys to. The signal my feed keeps.
stance: Self-custody yield beats any exchange that can freeze you at 4%.

hook: AI model releases went from yearly to monthly
caption: AI model releases used to be yearly events. Now it's every month. Blink and you miss a frontier launch — this is the release cadence my feed keeps for you.
stance: The release cycle is collapsing faster than anyone can keep up with.

hook: Monthly frontier models will force the whole industry faster
caption: Monthly frontier releases don't just ship models — they drag every lab into a faster loop. The pace is unbelievable. This is the AI signal my feed keeps before the timeline catches on.
stance: Monthly cadence is the new baseline — yearly labs are already behind.

hook: Frontier models now ship like continuous deployment
caption: Monthly frontier releases mean AI shipping now feels like CD — always-on, never a finished version. The release rhythm changed and most people missed it. The stuff my feed keeps for you.
stance: There's no 'latest model' anymore — just the current commit.

hook: What's actually inside OpenAI's o1? HN is trying to reverse-engineer it
caption: OpenAI shipped o1 and now Hacker News is dissecting the secret sauce behind its reasoning. Everyone wants to know what makes it tick. This is the thread my feed pulled before it scrolled away.

hook: Gemini Nano now runs AI on your Android — fully on-device
caption: Gemini Nano brings efficient on-device AI to Android — no cloud round-trip. The top 3 from Google I/O '25 for anyone building mobile. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: On-device beats the cloud the second your model fits in RAM.

hook: Siri finally gets the AI upgrade — everything from WWDC 2026
caption: Apple gave Siri the AI upgrade we've waited years for at WWDC 2026, alongside iOS 27. The full breakdown, found before the timeline.
stance: Apple is years late to AI Siri — the question is whether late still wins.

hook: Google's new TurboQuant AI just broke Two Minute Papers' brain
caption: Google's new TurboQuant AI is impressive enough to make Two Minute Papers say it broke his brain. Paper's out. The research my feed keeps before it trends.

hook: The fastest-growing AI repo this week is a list of the best AI tools
caption: awesome-evals by BenchFlow is the fastest-growing AI project right now. Curated eval resources devs actually star. Found before the timeline, because my feed reads the repos so you don't.
stance: The meta-tool that catalogs the tools always outgrows the tools.

hook: Baseten just shipped a framework for chaining multiple AI models
caption: Baseten launched Chains, an SDK for deploying complex multi-model AI products. If you're stitching models together in prod, this is for you. The infra my feed surfaces early.
stance: The next wave of AI apps is orchestration, not a single big model.

hook: They built an AI that reads game news so you don't
caption:
They built an AI that reads game news so you don't.

Auto-classifying gaming headlines is a real problem worth solving. This is the kind of build my feed surfaces before the timeline does.
stance: Manual news-scrolling for gamers is already dead; the tooling just hasn't caught up.

hook: Sony's already repurposing its PS disc factory
caption:
Rumor: Sony is repurposing its PlayStation disc factory, and Microsoft is lining up buyers for Xbox studios.

The consoles-are-dying signal, surfaced in my feed early.
stance: Physical media is over — Sony and Microsoft are just done pretending otherwise.

hook: A first-person liminal horror game from Studio Noori
caption: Farsight got its reveal trailer at the Horror Game Awards 2026 — first-person liminal horror from Studio Noori. If backrooms dread is your thing, this one's for you. Found before the timeline.

hook: Forget AAA vs indie. A fun game is a fun game.
caption: Forget AAA vs indie labels. There are more releases now than you'll ever finish — an enjoyable game is enjoyable, full stop. The takes my feed keeps over the timeline noise.
stance: The AAA/AA/indie distinction is marketing, not quality.

hook: 1 year of game dev, zero prior experience — use Godot.
caption: One year of game dev from zero, no experience — and the verdict is just use Godot. The open-source path most tutorials skip. The builder finds my feed keeps.
stance: You don't need Unity or Unreal to start — Godot is enough.

hook: Indie devs can't do $100 deluxe editions. Build a great game instead.
caption: Indie devs can't fund $100 deluxe editions, so stop expecting them to. Make a kick-ass product and players come. The dev-scene talk my feed surfaces before the timeline.
stance: Deluxe editions are a big-publisher tax; a great small game needs none of it.

hook: Steam and GoG made gamers stop caring about physical copies
caption: Steam and GoG got so trustworthy that all-digital libraries stopped scaring anyone. Most PC devs skip physical releases now. The shift my feed catches before the timeline argues about it.
stance: Physical PC releases are nostalgia, not preservation; trust killed the disc.

hook: A fresh batch of indie games dropped on Steam and Switch today
caption: A fresh batch of smaller indie titles just launched on Steam and Switch. The releases worth playing hide under the AAA noise. This is the stuff my feed keeps for you.

hook: An atmospheric driving game through Dutch dikes and windmills
caption: Lowlands Racer: an atmospheric driving game through Dutch polders, dikes and windmills, built in public. The oddly specific indie gems my feed finds before the timeline.

hook: Watch a pixel-art dungeon get its skill tree
caption: Watch a pixel-art dungeon grow its skill tree in real time. Solo devs building in the open are the best part of gaming. This gem surfaced before the timeline caught it.
stance: The best new releases are being built in public by one person right now.

hook: Your first favorite game may be gone forever
caption: Your first favorite mobile game might be unplayable now — pulled, delisted, gone. Game preservation is a real crisis. The stuff my feed surfaces that the timeline forgets.
stance: Delisting games is quietly erasing a whole generation's gaming history.

hook: A whole game about sledding just dropped on Game Pass.
caption: A whole game about sledding just dropped on Game Pass, and it looks absurdly fun. From a dev who clearly loves it. The kind of hidden release my feed keeps before you scroll past it.

hook: You don't need a Hollywood budget. You need to split the tasks.
caption: You don't need a Hollywood budget for game art, you need to know which tasks to hand AI and which to do by hand. A brutal-honest hybrid pipeline. The AI stuff my feed keeps.
stance: AI won't replace your art team, but knowing what to hand it will replace your budget.

hook: The "Godfather of NDEs" built a rational case for the afterlife
caption: The "Godfather of NDEs" built a rational worldview from near-death experiences, science, and philosophy. Fringe, or frontier? Weird-but-rigorous is exactly the stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The line between fringe metaphysics and real science is thinner than academics admit.

hook: Locke: kids learn more from a child's questions than a scholar's lectures
caption: Locke argued a curious child's questions teach more than educated men's borrowed opinions. Writing like this now gets called anti-intellectual. Old ideas, freshly dangerous. Found before the timeline.
stance: We started mistaking credentials for thinking, and Locke saw it coming.

hook: Reason or revelation, pick one. Just don't ask me to merge them.
caption: Reason or revelation, pick either. Just don't expect anyone to reconcile them for you. The cleanest take on faith vs logic I've seen all week. Found before the timeline.
stance: The demand to reconcile faith and reason is the trap, not the goal.

hook: Want to understand AI minds? Start with animal minds.
caption: Want to understand AI minds? Start with animal minds. A philosophy-of-mind syllabus that runs from behaviourism to consciousness. The heady stuff my feed keeps.
stance: You can't reason about machine consciousness without first reasoning about the animal kind.

hook: 2,000 years of Chinese political thought in one chart.
caption: 2,000 years of Chinese political thought in one hand-drawn chart. Daoism, Legalism, and every confusion you had, cleared up. Found before the timeline buried it.

hook: "I think I think too much."
caption: "I think I think too much." Six words that name the overthinker's trap: the mind auditing itself into paralysis. Small line, deep cut. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: Overthinking isn't deep thinking — it's thinking that never ships.

hook: De Beauvoir built an ethics with no rulebook
caption: De Beauvoir built an ethics with no rulebook — freedom without a safety net. If existentialism ever felt cold, this is the human read. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: Existentialism isn't nihilism — it's the most demanding ethics there is.

hook: DeFi has huge brand awareness — and almost zero users.
caption: DeFi has massive brand awareness and almost zero real usage. Everyone's heard of it; few actually touch it. The gap nobody wants to admit — surfaced from my curated feed.
stance: DeFi's hype-to-usage ratio is the real bubble.

hook: See the real historical yield of every DeFi protocol.
caption: Stop trusting the advertised APY — this tool plots the actual realized yield of DeFi protocols side by side. The receipts your dashboard hides. Found before the timeline.
stance: Advertised APY is marketing; realized yield is the truth.

hook: Another DeFi protocol just got hit. Do you know which?
caption: Another DeFi protocol got hit by a new security exploit. If you hold anything on-chain, the gap between the breach and the headline is where you get liquidated. Surfaced before the timeline knew.
stance: "Audited" means nothing — DeFi ships bugs to prod with your money as the test suite.

hook: The EU just voted to regulate your DeFi, staking and NFTs.
caption: The European Parliament advanced a resolution to assess DeFi, staking and NFTs — full plenary vote is scheduled. If you're on-chain in the EU, this shapes what's still legal. Found before the timeline.
stance: Regulators don't need to ban DeFi — they just regulate the fiat on-ramps you depend on.

hook: The paradox of DeFi: the more "decentralized," the more fragile.
caption: A new SSRN paper maps the paradox of DeFi — the features that make it trustless are the same ones that make it exploitable. If you build or invest on-chain, read the risk model. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: DeFi's decentralization isn't a safety feature — it's an attack surface nobody can patch fast enough.

hook: What actually happens when your game ships?
caption: What really happens the moment an indie game releases — is it all bugfixing hell, or more? A genuine question worth answering. The kind of thread my feed keeps.

hook: Agents of Groove just teased a full soundtrack overhaul.
caption: Agents of Groove just teased a full soundtrack and art overhaul. This is the indie release you want to watch before it blows up. The stuff my feed keeps before the hype hits.
